subject: UX & Research
title: Rage Clicks and Dead Clicks
description: A fast breakdown of Rage Clicks and Dead Clicks for builders.

hook.text: A user clicks the same button 7 times in 3 seconds. That's a rage click — pure digital frustration.

buildup.text: Rage clicks happen when users think something should respond but it doesn't. Broken buttons, slow loads.

discovery.text: Dead clicks land on non-interactive elements that look clickable. Text styled like links, static icons.

twist.text: Tools like FullStory and Hotjar auto-detect rage clicks. They're a free frustration radar with zero setup.

climax.text: Rage clicks map directly to broken expectations. Each one is a user telling you what needs fixing.

punchline.text: Rage clicks are frustration signals. Monitor and fix them weekly.

quiz.question: What does a rage click indicate about the user experience?
options: The user expects an element to respond but it doesn't — it signals broken expectations
The user is trying to select text on the page
